content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcer- PRACrrcEs CoMMrssroN
428 J Street r Suire 620 o Sacramenro, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322-0886
August 6,2009
Sandra L. McBrayer
4438 Ingraham St.
San Diego, California 92109
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-163
Dear Ms. McBrayer:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the honorarium
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act").1
QrrEsrroN
Are you prohibited by the Act from accepting payment as motivational speaker
while serving on the San Diego First Five Commission?
CONCLUSION
Yes, you are prohibited by the Act from accepting payment as a motivational
speaker while serving on the San Diego First Five commiision.
FACTS
You have been appointed by the San Diego County Board of Supervisors to serve
on the San Diego First Five Commission. In 1994 as the founding teacher of a public
school for homeless high school students you were named by Preiident Clintonas the
United States Teacher of the Year. You make several rpe""hes a year as the former
National Teacher of the Year speaking on issues conceming at-risl teenagers for which
you receive honoraria. For any speech you send a W9 wittr your personal income tax
number. These speeches are separate from your business as a CEb of a local non profit,
and you have been making such speeches for more than 15 years. you ask if you are
required to list the money you make from these motivational speeches o, youi Statement
of Economic Interests (Form 700). In a subsequent e-mail you stated that your position is
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Governmenr Code Sections g1000 through 91014. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8ll0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the california
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-09-163
Page No. 2
designated as "category l" in your agency's conflict-of-interest code. you did not
provide the description qssociated with category l, therefore, we assume you have full
disclosure requirements.2
ANALYSIS
Section 89503(a) prohibits a designated employee of a state agency from
accepting honoraria from any source, if the employie would be requiled to report the
receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interest.
"Honorarium" means . . . any payment made in consideration for any speech given,
article published, or attendance at any public or private conference, 
"onrerrtio"n, meeting,
social event, meal, or like gathering. (Section sgsoz(u).) However, ..honorarium,, does
not include:
". . . earned income for personal services which are customarily provided
in connection with the individual's business or practice of a Uona fiae
business, trade, or profession, such as teaching, practicing law, medicine,
insurance, real estate, banking, or building contracting, unless the sole or
predominant activity of the business, tradi, or profess-ion is making
speeches . . .. " (Section g9502(c).)
Your activity as a paid speaker is an activity covered by the honoraria
prohibitions. The funds you receive are payments made in consideration for a..speech
given." Thus we must consider whether your paid speaking activities fall within the
exemption of Section 89502(c). While it appears from youi lengthy work history that
your speaking may be a bona fide business, trade, or professionlRegutation 1g932.1),
businesses in which the "sole or predominant activity;'is making spfeches are excluded
from the exception. (Regulation 18932.2.) The sole activity of your business is making
speeches. Therefore your professional speaking is not exempted from the honoraria
prohibitions by Section 89502(c). The monies you receive for you^p.utinfui"
honoraria and are prohibited by the Act.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
'W+wK to"Pt"'
SKBjgl
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
.2 Because you are a member of the San Diego First Five Commission with a category I
designation we assume that your position requires full disclosure of all income. Thus, all in.or" you
receive is to be reported on your statement oi economic interests. (Section g7302(b).) If category I is not
full disc-lo-sure for your agency, you will need to determine if the source of the honorarium is i..r"po.tubl"
source." If it is not, the prohibition would not apply.

sections.conclusion:
Yes, you are prohibited by the Act from accepting payment as a motivational
speaker while serving on the San Diego First Five commiision.
